title: PlayStation puts an end to physical discs: protests, preservation debates and Xbox considers following the same path
summary: Sony's decision to remove physical support from its consoles generates reactions across the industry, from player protests to calls for digital preservation, as Microsoft evaluates abandoning discs in its next generation.

title: Capcom brings forward the release of Onimusha: Way of the Sword to September 4
summary: The action game will arrive three weeks early and the developers will offer gifts to early buyers.

title: The first Steam Machines hit the market and Shuhei Yoshida harshly criticizes them
summary: The former president of PlayStation tests the Steam Machine and compares it to the PS4, while problems with unauthorized accessories arise.

title: Frictional Games delays its new sci-fi game Ontos until 2027
summary: The studio behind Amnesia and Soma postpones its ambitious adventure to fulfill its creative vision.

title: Rumors of Obsidian Entertainment's closure are denied by reliable sources
summary: Speculation about the future of the Fallout: New Vegas studio causes confusion, but multiple reports indicate that there is no imminent risk.

title: Koei Tecmo shows the cinematic opening of Attack on Titan 3, which will arrive this winter
summary: The new game in the saga reveals its opening cinematic produced by MAPPA and confirms its release window.

title: Diablo 4 Season 14 brings back classic difficulty and offers new leveling guides
summary: Players celebrate the increased challenge as Polygon and 3DJuegos publish tips for fast progression.

title: OD, Hideo Kojima's horror game for Xbox, survives Microsoft's cuts
summary: New reports indicate that the exclusive title is safe from cancellation for the second time.

title: Valheim 1.0 will raise its price by 50% and will not modify the ocean
summary: The developers confirm that the ocean biome will not be updated and that the price will increase upon leaving early access.

title: Rockstar UK workers denounce gender pay gap and crunch on GTA 6
summary: Reports reveal problems with bonus management and working conditions at the studio.

title: The Godot engine bans AI-generated contributions
summary: After a controversy over AI-generated code, Godot requires that all contributions be written by humans.

title: The MMO Cinder City reduces its RAM requirement from 64 GB to 32 GB
summary: The initial 64 GB specification was considered excessive and the study reduces it by half.

title: Sony will close PS3 and PS Vita digital stores in July 2027
summary: Thousands of digital games will no longer be available when the platforms close their stores.

title: Leak suggests Final Fantasy 7 Revelation will have story DLC after the ending
summary: The game's ending would not be definitive according to new hints from a leak.

title: Flask, a hand-drawn alchemy autobattler, debuts a demo on Steam
summary: The auto-combat game with homunculi and goblin blood is now playable in a demo on Steam.

title: A Metroid Ravenous listing appears, sparking speculation about the series
summary: An age rating suggests that Nintendo could be preparing a new Metroid game.

title: Company of Heroes 3: Final Stand announced, a roguelite defense game for PC
summary: The war strategy saga will have a new wave-based title with roguelite elements, whose release is near.

title: Location guides in Forza Horizon 6: speed zones and treasures
summary: Polygon and PCGamer publish the locations of speed events and treasure hunts.

title: World of Warcraft fixes historical configuration issues and explains the chaotic April patch
summary: Blizzard implements quality of life improvements like auto-loot and details the causes of the bug that affected the update.

title: Pokémon's 30th anniversary cards spark controversy over their design and inflated prices
summary: The commemorative cards receive criticism for their appearance and for speculation in stores like GameStop.
